cases.question: Which companies in our database are independent — not owned by or part of any other company?

WHERE NOT EXISTS { (o)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
RETURN o.name AS company, o.description AS description

cases.question: How many news articles in our database are positive, neutral, or negative in tone? Give me a breakdown by sentiment category.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)
RETURN
  WHEN a.sentiment > 0 THEN 'positive'
       a.sentiment < 0 THEN 'negative'
  ELSE 'neutral' END AS sentimentCategory,
  count(a) AS articleCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC

cases.question: Among companies that are not subsidiaries of any other firm, which have the most negative media coverage on average? Only include companies with at least 3 articles.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o:Organization)
WHERE NOT EXISTS { (o)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
WITH o, avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment, count(a) AS articleCount
WHERE articleCount >= 3
RETURN o.name AS company, round(avgSentiment, 4) AS avgSentiment, articleCount
ORDER BY avgSentiment ASC
LIMIT 10

cases.question: For the 5 most-covered companies in the news, what was the most positively-toned article written about each of them?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
WITH o, count(a) AS articleCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC
LIMIT 5
CALL (o) {
  MATCH (o)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)
  WHERE a.sentiment IS NOT NULL
  RETURN a
  ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC
  LIMIT 1
}
RETURN o.name AS company, articleCount, a.title AS topPositiveArticle, a.sentiment AS sentiment, a.date AS articleDate
ORDER BY articleCount DESC

cases.question: Which 5 companies have the most total connections in our database — counting ownership relationships, news mentions, and industry category links combined?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
     COUNT { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }            AS ownershipLinks,
     COUNT { ()-[:MENTIONS]->(o) }                  AS mentionLinks,
     COUNT { (o)-[:IN_INDUSTRY|HAS_CATEGORY]->() }  AS industryLinks
WITH o,
     ownershipLinks + mentionLinks + industryLinks AS totalConnections,
     ownershipLinks, mentionLinks, industryLinks
ORDER BY totalConnections DESC
LIMIT 5
RETURN o.name AS company,
       totalConnections,
       ownershipLinks,
       mentionLinks,
       industryLinks

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

WITH o, count(a) AS articleCount, avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment

ORDER BY avgSentiment ASC

cases.question: Which company pairs appear in both positive and negative news stories? I'm looking for companies that generate mixed sentiment coverage together. Show the top 10 pairs.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o1:Organization)<-[:MENTIONS]-(a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o2:Organization)
WHERE o1.name < o2.name
WITH o1, o2,
     count(CASE WHEN a.sentiment > 0 THEN 1 END) AS posCount,
     count(CASE WHEN a.sentiment < 0 THEN 1 END) AS negCount
WHERE posCount > 0 AND negCount > 0
RETURN o1.name AS company1, o2.name AS company2,
       posCount, negCount, posCount + negCount AS totalArticles
ORDER BY totalArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

RETURN o.name AS company, articleCount, subsidiaryCount

cases.question: For companies that have at least one subsidiary, list each company's name alongside all the subsidiaries it directly owns and the industry categories it belongs to. Return up to 20 companies, ordered alphabetically.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WHERE EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) }
WITH o,
  COLLECT { MATCH (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(s:Organization) RETURN s.name ORDER BY s.name } AS subsidiaries,
  COLLECT { MATCH (o)-[:IN_INDUSTRY]->(ic:IndustryCategory) RETURN ic.name ORDER BY ic.name } AS industries
RETURN o.name AS company, subsidiaries, industries
ORDER BY o.name
LIMIT 20

cases.question: By industry category, give me a comprehensive picture: how many companies are in each category, how many news articles mention them in total, what is the average sentiment of that coverage, and how many subsidiaries exist across the group? Show the top 10 categories by total article count.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (cat:IndustryCategory)<-[:IN_INDUSTRY]-(org:Organization)
WITH cat, count(DISTINCT org) AS companyCount
CALL (cat) {
  MATCH (cat)<-[:IN_INDUSTRY]-(org:Organization)
  OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(org)
  RETURN count(DISTINCT a) AS totalArticles,
         avg(a.sentiment)   AS avgSentiment
}
CALL (cat) {
  MATCH (cat)<-[:IN_INDUSTRY]-(org:Organization)
  OPTIONAL MATCH (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
  RETURN count(DISTINCT sub) AS totalSubsidiaries
}
RETURN cat.name              AS category,
       companyCount,
       totalArticles,
       round(avgSentiment, 3) AS avgSentiment,
       totalSubsidiaries
ORDER BY totalArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Search for Microsoft in our company database. For each match, show its industry category, the top 3 news headlines about it, how many news articles mention it, and how many subsidiaries it has.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('entity', 'Microsoft') YIELD node AS org, score

WITH org, score,
     COUNT { (org)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) } AS subsidiaryCount,
     COUNT { (:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(org) }             AS articleCount

OPTIONAL MATCH (org)-[:IN_INDUSTRY]->(cat:IndustryCategory)

WITH org, score, subsidiaryCount, articleCount,
     collect(DISTINCT cat.name) AS industries

CALL (org) {
  MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(org)
  RETURN a.title AS headline
  ORDER BY a.date DESC
  LIMIT 3
}

WITH org, score, industries, articleCount, subsidiaryCount,
     collect(headline) AS topHeadlines

RETURN org.name      AS company,
       industries,
       topHeadlines,
       articleCount,
       subsidiaryCount
ORDER BY score DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: For companies with at least 5 subsidiaries, show the most positively-covered news article about each (if any), and list the names of companies that sit two levels down in their ownership hierarchy. Return up to 10 companies.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (parent:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
WITH parent, count(sub) AS subCount
WHERE subCount >= 5
CALL (parent) {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(parent)
  WITH a ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC LIMIT 1
  RETURN a AS topArticle
}
OPTIONAL MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(grandchild:Organization)
WITH parent, subCount, topArticle, collect(DISTINCT grandchild.name) AS grandchildren
RETURN parent.name           AS company,
       subCount               AS subsidiaryCount,
       topArticle.title       AS topArticleTitle,
       topArticle.sentiment   AS topArticleSentiment,
       grandchildren
ORDER BY subCount DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Classify every company into one of four groups: those with both subsidiaries and press coverage, those with subsidiaries but no press coverage, those with press coverage but no subsidiaries, and those with neither. How many companies fall into each group?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
  EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) } AS hasSubsidiaries,
  EXISTS { (:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o) }             AS hasCoverage
WITH CASE
  WHEN hasSubsidiaries AND hasCoverage          THEN 'subsidiaries + coverage'
  WHEN hasSubsidiaries AND NOT hasCoverage      THEN 'subsidiaries only'
  WHEN NOT hasSubsidiaries AND hasCoverage      THEN 'coverage only'
  ELSE                                               'neither'
END AS group
RETURN group, count(*) AS companyCount
ORDER BY companyCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
